title: Synostemon hubbardii

intro: Synostemon hubbardii, commonly known as the Australian sage or bush sage, is a shrubby plant native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Synostemon hubbardii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Synostemon hubbardii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken to relieve symptoms of respiratory infections and to promote general well-being.
